subject: Chemistry
title: Greek Four Elements Theory
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Greek Four Elements Theory.

hook.text: Empedocles proposed that everything is made of four elements: earth, water, air, and fire.

buildup.text: Aristotle refined the idea, adding that elements had qualities: hot, cold, wet, and dry in different combinations.

discovery.text: This framework dominated for 2,000 years. If elements could change qualities, one could transform into another.

twist.text: The logic was simple: if lead and gold differ only in qualities, rearranging those qualities would turn lead into gold.

climax.text: This idea was wrong—but it inspired centuries of experiments. Alchemists accidentally discovered real chemistry.

punchline.text: Four elements, wrong theory, 2,000 years of experiments. Errors drove progress.

quiz.question: Who proposed that all matter is made of earth, water, air, and fire?
options: Plato, in his theory of forms
